---
title: 'Don&#39;t Look Back... Jesus is Coming!'
date: '2010-03-26T18:52:00.0000000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: 'Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '7737773116187406858'
blogger_filename: '/2010/03/don-look-back-jesus-is-coming.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller/feed.atom'
---

<a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Luke%2017&amp;version=NKJV">Luke 17</a><br /><br /><sup class="versenum" id="en-NKJV-25668"></sup><sup class="versenum" id="en-NKJV-25668">20</sup> Now when He was asked by the Pharisees when the kingdom of God would come, He answered them and said, <b>“The kingdom of God does not come with observation;</b> <sup class="versenum" id="en-NKJV-25669">21</sup> nor will they say, ‘See here!’ or ‘See there!’<sup class="footnote" value="[&lt;a href=&quot;#fen-NKJV-25669d&quot; title=&quot;See footnote d&quot;&gt;d&lt;/a&gt;]">[<a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Luke%2017&amp;version=NKJV#fen-NKJV-25669d" title="See footnote d">d</a>]</sup> For indeed, the kingdom of God is within you.”    <br \="" /> <sup class="versenum" id="en-NKJV-25670">22</sup> Then He said to the disciples, “The days will come when you will desire to see one of the days of the Son of Man, and you will not see <i>it.</i> <sup class="versenum" id="en-NKJV-25671">23</sup> And they will say to you, ‘Look here!’ or ‘Look there!’<sup class="footnote" value="[&lt;a href=&quot;#fen-NKJV-25671e&quot; title=&quot;See footnote e&quot;&gt;e&lt;/a&gt;]">[<a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Luke%2017&amp;version=NKJV#fen-NKJV-25671e" title="See footnote e">e</a>]</sup> Do not go after <i>them</i> or follow <i>them.</i> <sup class="versenum" id="en-NKJV-25672">24</sup> <b>For as the lightning that flashes out of one <i>part</i> under heaven shines to the other <i>part</i> under heaven, so also the Son of Man will be in His day</b>. <sup class="versenum" id="en-NKJV-25673">25</sup> But first He must suffer many things and be rejected by this generation. <sup class="versenum" id="en-NKJV-25674">26</sup> And as it was in the days of Noah, so it will be also in the days of the Son of Man: <sup class="versenum" id="en-NKJV-25675">27</sup> They ate, they drank, they married wives, they were given in marriage, until the day that Noah entered the ark, and the flood came and destroyed them all. <sup class="versenum" id="en-NKJV-25676">28</sup> Likewise as it was also in the days of Lot: They ate, they drank, they bought, they sold, they planted, they built; <sup class="versenum" id="en-NKJV-25677">29</sup> but on the day that Lot went out of Sodom it rained fire and brimstone from heaven and <b>destroyed <i>them</i> all. <sup class="versenum" id="en-NKJV-25678">30</sup> Even so will it be in the day when the Son of Man is revealed.    </b><br \="" /> <sup class="versenum" id="en-NKJV-25679">31</sup> “In that day, he who is on the housetop, and his goods <i>are</i> in the house, let him not come down to take them away. And likewise the one who is in the field, let him not turn back. <b><sup class="versenum" id="en-NKJV-25680">32</sup> Remember Lot’s wife.</b> <sup class="versenum" id="en-NKJV-25681">33</sup> Whoever seeks to save his life will lose it, and whoever loses his life will preserve it. <sup class="versenum" id="en-NKJV-25682">34</sup> I tell you, in that night there will be two <i>men</i> in one bed: the one will be <b>taken</b> and the other will be left. <sup class="versenum" id="en-NKJV-25683">35</sup> Two <i>women</i> will be grinding together: the one will be <b>taken</b> and the other left. <sup class="versenum" id="en-NKJV-25684">36</sup> Two <i>men</i> will be in the field: the one will be <b>taken</b> and the other left.”<sup class="footnote" value="[&lt;a href=&quot;#fen-NKJV-25684f&quot; title=&quot;See footnote f&quot;&gt;f&lt;/a&gt;]">[<a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Luke%2017&amp;version=NKJV#fen-NKJV-25684f" title="See footnote f">f</a>]</sup><br \="" /> <sup class="versenum" id="en-NKJV-25685">37</sup> And they answered and said to Him, “<b>Where</b>, Lord?”    <br \="" />So He said to them, <b>“Wherever the body is, there the eagles will be gathered together.”</b><br /><br /><br /><span style="font-size: small;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; This set of passages intrigued me deeply. I have just been listening to a teaching from <a href="http://charlescapps.com/">Charles Caps</a> from June 2002 on <a href="http://www.bvov.tv/emic/ondemand_archive.php">The Solomon Concept</a> and end times prophecy. He caused me to rethink some of what I know about end times prophecy and how the scriptures paint those days vs how people have said the scriptures paint those days.&nbsp; </span><br /><br /><span style="font-size: small;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; I asked myself several questions about this passage. The main one I was curious about is that it seemed to me at face level this passage contradicted something he said about the rapture NOT taking place in the twinkling of an eye but the changing of our bodies would take place in the twinkling of an eye and we would be here for 40 days after. It's very interesting. But doesn't this verse speak of the rapture? I've quoted this verse as a rapture verse several times. </span><br /><span style="font-size: small;"><br /></span><br /><span style="font-size: small;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Answer: NO It doesn't.&nbsp;</span><br /><span style="font-size: small;"><br /></span><br /><span style="font-size: small;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Where then? His disciples asked? Where will those taken be taken? They will be gathered like wheat at His Second coming, which is after the rapture and not until the end of the tribulation. The Holy Spirit had me compare his wording in this passage:&nbsp;</span><br /><span style="font-size: small;"><br /></span><br /><ul><li><span style="font-size: small;">Everyone Sees Him</span></li><li><span style="font-size: small;">Heaven opened and Lightening in the sky </span></li><li><span style="font-size: small;">HIS day</span></li><li><span style="font-size: small;">Comparing destruction of evil places to His coming</span></li><li><span style="font-size: small;">Gathering People</span></li><li><span style="font-size: small;">The BODY refers to HIS body which will come WITH Him at the end.</span></li></ul><span style="font-size: small;">To This passage in Revelations: </span><br /><br />&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Revelations%2019:%2011-21&amp;version=NKJV">Revelations 19</a><br /><br /><br /><sup class="versenum" id="en-NKJV-31025">11</sup> Now I saw <b>heaven opened</b>, and behold, a white horse. And <b>He who sat on him <i>was</i> called Faithful and True, and in righteousness He judges and makes war.</b><br /><sup class="versenum" id="en-NKJV-31028">14</sup> And the <b>armies in heaven, clothed in fine linen, white and clean,<sup class="footnote" value="[&lt;a href=&quot;#fen-NKJV-31028b&quot; title=&quot;See footnote b&quot;&gt;b&lt;/a&gt;]">[<a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Revelations%2019:%2011-21&amp;version=NKJV#fen-NKJV-31028b" title="See footnote b">b</a>]</sup> followed</b> Him on white horses.<br /><sup class="versenum" id="en-NKJV-31035">21</sup> And<b> the rest were killed with the sword which proceeded from the mouth of Him who sat on the horse</b>. And all the birds were filled with their flesh.<br /><br /><br /><h4><a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=2%20Thessalonians+1&amp;version=NKJV">2 Thessalonians 1</a></h4><h4><sup class="versenum" id="en-NKJV-29649" style="font-weight: normal;">3</sup><span style="font-weight: normal;"> We are bound to thank God always for you, brethren, as it is fitting, because your faith grows exceedingly, and the love of every one of you all abounds toward each other, </span><sup class="versenum" id="en-NKJV-29650" style="font-weight: normal;">4</sup><span style="font-weight: normal;"> so that we ourselves boast of you among the churches of God for your patience and faith in all your persecutions and tribulations that you endure, </span><sup class="versenum" id="en-NKJV-29651" style="font-weight: normal;">5</sup><span style="font-weight: normal;"> </span><i style="font-weight: normal;">which is</i><span style="font-weight: normal;"> manifest evidence of the righteous judgment of God, that you may be counted worthy of the kingdom of God, for which you also suffer; </span><sup class="versenum" id="en-NKJV-29652" style="font-weight: normal;">6</sup><span style="font-weight: normal;"> since </span><i style="font-weight: normal;">it is</i><span style="font-weight: normal;"> a righteous thing with God to repay with tribulation those who trouble you, </span><sup class="versenum" id="en-NKJV-29653" style="font-weight: normal;">7</sup><span style="font-weight: normal;"> and to </span><i style="font-weight: normal;">give</i><span style="font-weight: normal;"> you who are troubled rest with us <b>when the Lord Jesus is revealed from heaven with His mighty angels, </b></span><sup class="versenum" id="en-NKJV-29654" style="font-weight: normal;">8</sup><span style="font-weight: normal;"> <b>in flaming fire taking vengeance on those who do not know God, and on those who do not obey the gospel of our Lord Jesus Christ. </b></span><b><sup class="versenum" id="en-NKJV-29655" style="font-weight: normal;">9</sup><span style="font-weight: normal;"> T</span></b><span style="font-weight: normal;"></span><span style="font-weight: normal;">hese shall be punished with everlasting </span><b><span style="font-weight: normal;">destruction from the presence of the Lord and from the glory of His power, </span><sup class="versenum" id="en-NKJV-29656" style="font-weight: normal;">10</sup><span style="font-weight: normal;"> when He comes,</span></b><span style="font-weight: normal;"></span><span style="font-weight: normal;"> in that Day, to be glorified in His saints and to be admired among all those who believe,</span><sup class="footnote" style="font-weight: normal;" value="[&lt;a href=&quot;#fen-NKJV-29656a&quot; title=&quot;See footnote a&quot;&gt;a&lt;/a&gt;]">[<a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=2%20Thessalonians+1&amp;version=NKJV#fen-NKJV-29656a" title="See footnote a">a</a>]</sup><span style="font-weight: normal;"> because our testimony among you was believed.</span><b><span style="font-weight: normal;">    </span></b><br \="" style="font-weight: normal;" /> <sup class="versenum" id="en-NKJV-29657" style="font-weight: normal;">11</sup><span style="font-weight: normal;"> T<b>herefore we also pray always for you that our God would count you worthy of</b> </span><i><b><span style="font-weight: normal;">t</span><span style="font-weight: normal;">his</span></b></i><span style="font-weight: normal;"> <b>calling</b>, and fulfill all the good pleasure of </span><i style="font-weight: normal;">His</i><span style="font-weight: normal;"> goodness and the work of faith </span><span style="font-weight: normal;">with power, </span><sup class="versenum" id="en-NKJV-29658" style="font-weight: normal;">12</sup><span style="font-weight: normal;"> that the name of our Lord Jesus Christ may be glorified in you, and </span><span style="font-weight: normal;">you in Him, according to the grace of our God and the Lord Jesus Christ.</span> </h4><h4>&nbsp;</h4>Therefore this passage is talking about the last day, the day when Jesus returns to set right all things for his thousand year reign to begin. It is not talking about the rapture. Which means an entire paradigm shift regarding end times thought. There are very interesting things to come.<br /><br /><br />&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; OK... so what? What does this mean to me right now? I think the biggest admonition here is to remember Lot's wife. DON'T LOOK BACK!<br /><br /><br />&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Do not look back at past years decades or minutes. Look forward. What does God have for you? What are His plans for you? Is what you're doing right now taking you toward his plan or away from it? There is no neutral.<br /><br />&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Are you making plans for later this year that God would totally LOVE to turn upside down if you'd let Him? God wants me living independant of any "JOB" and living in a house fully furnished, finished, full and free before 2011. Will I let Him do that? Will I break my own mindsets and plow the ground of my heart to allow my mindset to expand to include TOO BIG DREAMS?<br /><br />&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Jesus is coming soon! We have no time to wait and wait for nothing to happen because we were too scared to take His plan and run with it. I will take steps toward that freedom. It starts with consistency here in this journal. It then moves to completing what I started with KOG Concepts. What's next? I'll get that done and let you know.<br /><br />Darrell G. Wolfe<br /><br />PS... Charles pointed to the scripture and said that we WILL know the day of the rapture as the day approaches. The time will not be a suprise to us at all? Throw your head for a loop? Click on the link to hear it for yourself. You be the judge:<br /><br /><a href="mms://stream.kcmmedia.org/emicod/audio/e20020630-a.wma">The Solomon Concept</a>
